qualifying_org_definition: Any organization or individual for whom the recipient performs unpaid work and who can verify the hours. 10 CCR 2506-1-4.311 lists 'Unpaid work verified by the provider of the unpaid work' as a way to meet the 20 hrs/week (80 hrs/month) requirement, with no restriction on organization type and no county-approved-site registry. The state Volunteer Verification Form labels the organization field 'Organization or Agency Name (if applicable)' — i.e. even an individual supervisor qualifies. Colorado Workfare at county-designated sites (4.311.A.4) is a separate, optional lane.
qualifyingOrgShort: Any organization or individual the recipient does unpaid work for who will verify the hours; no in-state, registration, or county-approved-site requirement.
verification_instrument: ABAWD Volunteer Verification Form (titled 'Volunteer Verification Form' on the form itself; CDHS refers to it as the 'ABAWD Volunteer Verification Form', hosted via cdhs.colorado.gov/snap/abawd). Filled out by the organization/individual the recipient volunteers for; submitted ONE time during the SNAP certification period. Submittable via Colorado PEAK portal or MyCOBenefits app upload, or returned to the county office in person or by mail.
reportingCadence: Once per SNAP certification period (not monthly). Per CDHS: 'This is only required to be submitted one time during your SNAP certification period.'
hours_formula_detail: General unpaid-volunteer lane = flat 80 hrs/month (20 hrs/week). 10 CCR 2506-1-4.311 and the Feb 2026 (Version 10) state training desk-aid both list 'Volunteer 20 hours per week or 80 hours per month' as a distinct compliance option, and the CBMS data-entry option is 'In-kind/volunteer work, avg. of 80 hrs/month.' The state Volunteer Verification Form asks: 'Is the individual volunteering an average of 20 hours/week or 80 hours/month?' The benefit-divided-by-minimum-wage formula applies ONLY to the separate, optional 'Colorado Workfare' program (4.311.A.4) at county-designated sites, not to the general volunteer lane. (For reference, CO state minimum wage is $15.16/hr in 2026; some localities higher — Denver $19.29 — but this affects only the workfare lane, not the 80-hour general lane.)
formulaDetail: N/A for the general volunteer lane — flat 80 hrs/month. The allotment-divided-by-minimum-wage formula applies only to the separate optional Colorado Workfare program (4.311.A.4) at county-designated sites; CO 2026 state minimum wage used there would be $15.16/hr (higher local rates apply in some cities, e.g. Denver $19.29).
accepts_outofstate_detail: Neither 10 CCR 2506-1-4.311 nor the Volunteer Verification Form imposes any restriction on the location or registration status of the verifying organization. The form requests only volunteer name, CBMS case number, start date, nature of work, supervisor/individual name, 'Organization or Agency Name (if applicable)', phone, email, and signature — no EIN, no in-state address, no Colorado charitable-registration requirement, no notarization. Federal default (7 CFR 273.24) permits any qualifying provider; absent an explicit state restriction, out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be accepted operationally, but the state is formally silent.
geographic_waivers: none in FY2026 — ABAWD enforced statewide. The USDA FNS FY2025-2029 ABAWD waiver page lists Colorado only with a FY 2025 waiver (FNS response 08/15/2024), which covered federal FY2025 (through 09/30/2025) and does not extend into FY2026. OBBBA (HR1, 2025) eliminated the insufficient-jobs/high-unemployment standard waiver authority; remaining waiver authority is limited to areas with unemployment above 10% or noncontiguous states (AK/HI). USDA moved to terminate existing time-limit waivers around Nov 2, 2025 (with some terminated waivers kept in effect through their original expiration pending Rhode Island District Court litigation). No FY2026 Colorado ABAWD waiver is on file, so no Colorado geographic waiver is in effect for FY2026.
geographicWaivers: None in FY2026. FNS FY2025-2029 waiver page lists only a Colorado FY2025 waiver (response 08/15/2024), expired with FY2025. OBBBA removed the insufficient-jobs/high-unemployment waiver authority (now limited to >10% unemployment areas or noncontiguous AK/HI). No FY2026 Colorado waiver on file; ABAWD enforced statewide.
submissionMechanism: Colorado PEAK online portal upload, MyCOBenefits mobile app upload, in-person at the county human-services office, or by mail. The completed form (signed by the organization/individual volunteered for) is submitted once per certification period.
submission_deadline: Once per SNAP certification period; no specific monthly deadline. The form is submitted one time during the certification period to document compliance (typically at recertification or when compliance is documented).
submissionDeadline: Once per SNAP certification period (no monthly deadline); submitted one time during the certification period.
notes:
FY2026 VERIFICATION PASS (2026-06-22): Confirmed CO classification = federal_80 against multiple primary sources retrieved THIS pass. (1) Official CDHS ABAWD page (fetched via browser UA; WebFetch 403'd): 'ABAWDs must participate in a work activity 20 hours a week (80 hours a month)' and '...can include paid work, unpaid work (volunteer)...'; volunteer hours documented via the 'ABAWD Volunteer Verification Form,' 'only required to be submitted one time during your SNAP certification period.' Age range now 18-64 (OBBBA expansion). (2) 10 CCR 2506-1-4.311 (verified via both LII and the authoritative Colorado SOS regulation PDF, 1.9 MB): the 80-hour requirement is met by 'Unpaid work verified by the provider of the unpaid work'; 'Participating in and complying with the Colorado Workfare program' is a SEPARATE fourth option, not the general volunteer lane. The allotment-over-minimum-wage formula belongs only to the optional Colorado Workfare lane (county-designated sites). (3) Feb 2026 (Version 10) state training desk-aid lists 'Volunteer 20 hours per week or 80 hours per month' as a distinct option and the CBMS entry 'In-kind/volunteer work, avg. of 80 hrs/month.' (4) The actual Volunteer Verification Form PDF (Google Drive 1Zos7-h0EkU-4L-oJ1bFKKCHTz9m98S2Z) was retrieved and its text extracted: title 'Volunteer Verification Form,' compliance question 'Is the individual volunteering an average of 20 hours/week or 80 hours/month? Yes/No,' fields = CBMS case #, volunteer name, start date, nature of work, individual/supervisor name, 'Organization or Agency Name (if applicable),' phone, email, signature, date. No EIN, no in-state requirement, no notarization, no county-approved-site box — supporting out-of-state = silent (no restriction imposed) and prestep = none.

WAIVERS / ENFORCEMENT (FY2026): FNS FY2025-2029 waiver page lists Colorado ONLY with a FY2025 waiver (response 08/15/2024); no FY2026 Colorado entry. A FY2025 waiver expired with federal FY2025 (09/30/2025). OBBBA (HR1, 2025) eliminated the insufficient-jobs/high-unemployment standard waiver authority (remaining authority limited to >10% unemployment areas or noncontiguous AK/HI); USDA moved to terminate existing time-limit waivers ~Nov 2, 2025, with some kept through original expiration pending Rhode Island District Court litigation. Net: no Colorado geographic ABAWD waiver in effect for FY2026; ABAWD enforced statewide. NOTE: an earlier search snippet referencing 'Denver metro counties have active ABAWD waivers' reflected the PRIOR-YEAR (FY2024/FY2025) waiver, now lapsed — it is NOT a FY2026 waiver.

REMOTE STANCE: silent — neither the regulation, the CDHS page, the desk-aid, nor the form addresses remote/virtual volunteering. (Prior file had 'unknown'; normalized to 'silent' since the state does not prohibit it but also does not address it.)

COUNTY VARIATION: Colorado SNAP is county-administered (CBMS statewide system); the volunteer lane and form are state-issued and uniform, but submission logistics (which county office, local PEAK/MyCOBenefits assistance) vary by county. countyVariation kept true.

CONFIDENCE: high — classification (federal_80), hours (80/month), and form (ABAWD Volunteer Verification Form) are all directly confirmed by the governing state regulation, the official CDHS page, the Feb 2026 state training desk-aid, AND the actual form PDF retrieved this pass.

PRIOR-PASS NOTES (retained): Adversarial review for hidden prestep/EDP/approved-site registry found none — 4.311.A.1.a.iii permits any provider-verified unpaid work; 4.311 preamble accepts client statement as verification unless questionable; Employment First is an alternative path, not a mandatory prestep; section 4.310 work registration (signing the application) is universal SNAP registration, not an ABAWD-volunteer gate.
conflictsWithPrior: remote_stance: prior file 'unknown' -> normalized to 'silent' (state does not address remote volunteering but does not prohibit it).

geographic_waivers wording: prior file cited 'FNA ABAWD waivers page lists only a FY 2025 waiver (08/15/2024)'; this pass confirms that entry and adds OBBBA context (insufficient-jobs waiver authority eliminated; existing waivers terminated ~Nov 2, 2025 with RI-litigation carve-through) — same conclusion (no FY2026 CO waiver, enforced statewide), more precise sourcing.

minor: prior file did not carry the explicit federal_80 'classification' field name or the 2026 state minimum wage ($15.16) reference for the workfare lane; added for completeness. No substantive classification change.
unresolved: Remote/virtual volunteering eligibility is not addressed in any Colorado primary source (regulation, CDHS page, training desk-aid, or form) — treated as 'silent'.

Out-of-state 501(c)(3) acceptance is not explicitly stated; inferred 'silent/likely-allowed' because the form and regulation impose no in-state or registration requirement, but no source affirmatively confirms cross-state providers are accepted.

OBBBA waiver implementation memo and Ballotpedia OBBBA page are JS-rendered and could not be fully extracted via fetch; OBBBA waiver details corroborated via WebSearch synthesis + FNS waiver-page Colorado entry rather than a single quoted memo paragraph.
